zh.lifeSummary: 當John Pêche於1255年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓時，他的父親Gilbert de Pecche35歲，母親Maud Hastings34歲。他和Margaret Moyne至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1296年5月14日在英國英格蘭享年41歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾。

nameMeanings.name: Pêche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a variant of   Pech   ‘pitch, tar’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Peče or of its Germanized form Petsche , which is also a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname.

variants.name: Pech
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Alvaro, Diego, Edelmira, Emilio, Francisco, Gilberto, Jorge, Jose Angel.  German Gerhard, Hans, Ilse, Lutz, Manfred, Wolf.

Czech and Slovak; Sorbian (Pěch and P'ech): from a pet form of the personal name Petr (Czech), Pětš (Lower Sorbian), and Peter (Slovak). This is also a Germanized form of the Upper Sorbian cognate Pjech.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German pech (German Pech) ‘pitch, tar’, presumably a nickname for someone with exceptionally dark skin or hair, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who prepared, sold, or used pitch. This may in part also be the origin of a homonymic Polish, Czech, and Slovak surname (compare above and below).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When John Pêche was born in 1255, in Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Gilbert de Pecche, was 35 and his mother, Maud Hastings, was 34. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Margaret Moyne. He died on 14 May 1296, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41, and was buried in Warwickshire, England.

Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Margaret Moyne. Il est décédé le 14 mai 1296, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: John Pêcheが1255年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウオルムレイトンで生まれた時，父Gilbert de Peccheは35歳，母Maud Hastingsは34歳であった。彼はMargaret Moyneとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1296年5月14日にイギリスイギリスで41歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Pêche(이)가 1255년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워레이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert de Pecche(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Hastings(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Margaret Moyne(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1296년 5월 14일 영국 잉글랜드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르에 묻혔다.
